danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple hostile NPC sprites filling the screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat engagement to progress

danger_signals.signal: Closed door at top of room
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked until sufficient enemies defeated

danger_signals.visual_form: Different visual weapon equipment on enemy sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; varied attack patterns expected

safety_signals.signal: Open door at top of room
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage to next level; enemies sufficiently cleared

safety_signals.visual_form: Active protagonist sprite responsive to input
safety_signals.meaning: Agency maintained; immediate danger avoidable through player action

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rescue mission narrative (Princess Min-Min as goal marker)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied weapon equipment distinguishes enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Captive princess and stolen treasures as invasion markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sorrow/despair atmosphere; player characters must recover stolen items
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy warriors guard territory; must be defeated in sequence to unlock progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Door unlocks at top of screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can exit and proceed to next area without combat resistance
Optional combat encounters with potential treasure rewards

environmental_storytelling.example: Princess Min-Min's captivity and 10 stolen treasures establish stakes and progression reward structure
Each room filled with 'countless enemy warriors' signifies hostile occupation of the nameless land
Door unlocking after enemy defeat creates visual metaphor for clearing obstacles to rescue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attack player; block progression; vary attack patterns based on equipped weapons
Must defeat enemies systematically; return from training implies increased capability
Opens only after sufficient enemy count defeated; provides clear completion feedback

notes: Source material (Wikipedia) provides minimal visual/manifestation detail. Extraction based on gameplay description and narrative structure. Game uses classical beat-em-up world-state communication: enemy density = danger level, progression gates = task completion feedback, equipment variation = threat scaling. No explicit faction color schemes or detailed environmental markers documented in source. Political situation is simplified rescue narrative (good vs evil) with treasure recovery as secondary objective. Visual communication likely relies on sprite variation and door state changes typical of NES-era action games.
